name: Fried Apples
directions: Melt butter in saucepan and add remainder of ingredients.
Simmer until apples are tender about 20-25 minutes .

name: Candy Apples
directions: Wash and dry apples; remove stems.
Insert wooden sticks into stem end of each apple; set aside.
Combine next 4 ingredients in a heavy saucepan; mix well.
Cook over medium heat until mixture reaches 295 degrees (candy thermometer), stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat; stir in food coloring.
Quickly dip apples into mixture; allow excess to drip off.
Place apples on a buttered baking sheet to cool.

name: Apple Surprise
directions: Using an apple corer or a knife and cut out the inside of an apple.
Remove the core and all apple seeds.
Fill the apple with peanut butter or a mixture of peanut butter and raisins. MMM-good!

name: Apple-Banana Fruit Leather
directions: Blend ingredients in blender until pureed.
Dry in dehydrator for 7 1/2 hours at 125 degrees.

name: Fresh Applesauce
directions: Pour water and lemon juice in a bowl.
Add sliced apples and let stand 15 minutes.
Combine spice, honey, and half the apple slices in a blender.
Blend, adding a balance of apples until all have been blended until smooth.
Chill before serving.

name: Fruit Leather (Apple)
directions: Preheat oven to 150 degrees.
Mix the first 3 ingredients together.
Spray cookie sheet.
Spread a THIN layer of the mixture on the cookie sheet.
Leave oven door open a bit and cook for 6-8 hours.

name: Fruit Leather
directions: Place all ingredients in blender; blend to puree. Line 2 or 3 cookie sheets with plastic wrap or spray with non-stick vegetable spray. Spread puree evenly, leaving a 1 inch wide border all around. Dry in oven at lowest possible temperature (under 200 degrees) until no longer moist and sticky. Peel off plastic wrap; roll up. Slice at intervals and wrap in plastic wrap to store.

name: Banana Applesauce
directions: Peel, quarter, core and slice apples.  Chop bananas.  Combine both in in a large saucepan; add remaining ingredients.

Bring to a boil; cover and simmer for 5 minutes or until apple is pulpy.  Reduce heat to low or medium and whisk/stir until pretty well mashed; remove from heat and continue whisking/mashing until it is as smooth as you like it.
